text: The surgeon uses an Alexis wound retractor to safely reduce the exteriorized bowel through a small incision, enhancing visibility and access to the surgical site.
After mobilizing the colon and ligating the ileocolic pedicle, the surgeon exteriorizes the bowel through a small midline laparotomy wound, which extends from the umbilical port site.
An Alexis wound retractor is positioned in the midline laparotomy wound, providing protection and retraction for the exteriorised bowel.

The surgeon returns the resected bowel to the abdomen by unfolding the white ring of the Alexis wound retractor, allowing the bowel to fill the Alexis tunnel and settle back into the abdominal cavity.
The surgeon twists the alexis wound retractor to create a seal around the exteriorised bowel, ensuring it remains securely within the retractor tunnel.
Gentle pressure is applied to the exteriorized bowel, allowing it to slide smoothly through the Alexis wound retractor tunnel back into the abdominal cavity.
The surgeon untwists the Alexis wound retractor and removes the green ring from the abdominal cavity, completing the exteriorization process.
The surgeon closes the wound using standard suturing techniques after the bowel has been returned to the abdomen.
